                        politics?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                        scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                        statuses?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                    };
                }[];
                timeUnit: { plural: string; singular: string };
            };
        }
        • Optionalaborted?: boolean
        • Optionalassumptions?: string[]

          Assumptions held true during the simulation.

        • Optionalcitations?: { context: string; doi?: string; text: string; url: string }[]
        • Optionalcost?: {
              breakdown?: Record<string, number>;
              cachedReadTokens?: number;
              cacheSavingsUSD?: number;
              inputTokens?: number;
              llmCalls?: number;
              outputTokens?: number;
              totalUSD: number;
          }
          • Optionalbreakdown?: Record<string, number>

            Per-site / per-model breakdown: { director: 0.12, departments: 0.34 }.

          • OptionalcachedReadTokens?: number
          • OptionalcacheSavingsUSD?: number
          • OptionalinputTokens?: number
          • OptionalllmCalls?: number
          • OptionaloutputTokens?: number
          • totalUSD: number
        • Optionaldecisions?: {
              actor?: string;
              choice: string;
              outcome?:
                  | "risky_success"
                  | "risky_failure"
                  | "conservative_success"
                  | "conservative_failure";
              rationale?: string;
              reasoning?: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              time: number;
          }[]

          Every decision made during the run — one per commander choice in turn-loop.

        • Optionaldisclaimer?: string

          Scenario-supplied disclaimer (digital-twin simulations use this for medical / professional caveats).

        • OptionalfinalState?: {
              capacities?: Record<string, number>;
              environment?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>;
              metrics: Record<string, number>;
              politics?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              statuses?: Record<string, string | boolean>;
          }
          • Optionalcapacities?: Record<string, number>

            Capacity constraints: life support, housing, budget.

          • Optionalenvironment?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>

            Environmental conditions: weather, radiation, depth, altitude.

          • metrics: Record<string, number>

            Numeric gauges: food, power, population, morale, VO2max, whatever.

          • Optionalpolitics?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>

            Political/social pressures. Values may be numeric or categorical.

          • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
          • Optionalstatuses?: Record<string, string | boolean>

            Categorical state: governance status, alignment, phase.

        • OptionalfinalSwarm?: {
              agents: {
                  age?: number;
                  agentId: string;
                  alive: boolean;
                  childrenIds?: string[];
                  department: string;
                  featured?: boolean;
                  generation?: number;
                  marsborn?: boolean;
                  mood?: string;
                  name: string;
                  partnerId?: string;
                  psychScore?: number;
                  rank?: "junior" | "senior" | "lead" | "chief";
                  role: string;
                  shortTermMemory?: string[];
              }[];
              births?: number;
              deaths?: number;
              morale?: number;
              population: number;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              time: number;
              turn: number;
          }

          Final agent-swarm roster at end-of-run. Pairs with finalState: the world snapshot is the macro view (metrics, statuses), the swarm snapshot is the micro view (every agent's role, mood, family edges, memory). Populated in turn-loop runs that exercised the swarm. Use WorldModel.swarm() for the live equivalent during a run.

          • agents: {
                age?: number;
                agentId: string;
                alive: boolean;
                childrenIds?: string[];
                department: string;
                featured?: boolean;
                generation?: number;
                marsborn?: boolean;
                mood?: string;
                name: string;
                partnerId?: string;
                psychScore?: number;
                rank?: "junior" | "senior" | "lead" | "chief";
                role: string;
                shortTermMemory?: string[];
            }[]

            Every agent in the swarm at snapshot time, alive or dead.

          • Optionalbirths?: number

            Number of births this turn.

          • Optionaldeaths?: number

            Number of deaths this turn.

          • Optionalmorale?: number

            Aggregate morale 0..1 (population-weighted).

          • population: number

            Aggregate counts derived from agents.

          • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
          • time: number

            Scenario time at snapshot (years/quarters/ticks per scenario).

          • turn: number

            Turn number this snapshot was taken at (0-indexed).

        • Optionalfingerprint?: Record<string, string | number>

          Loose classification scores. Paracosm heritage; scenarios may extend.

        • OptionalforgedTools?: {
              approved: boolean;
              confidence?: number;
              department?: string;
              description?: string;
              name: string;
          }[]
        • Optionalintervention?: {
              adherenceProfile?: { expected: number; risks?: string[] };
              category?: string;
              description: string;
              duration?: { unit: string; value: number };
              id: string;
              mechanism?: string;
              name: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              targetBehaviors?: string[];
          }

          Intervention being tested on the subject.

        • OptionalleveragePoints?: string[]

          Actionable leverage points for consumers of the artifact.

        • metadata: {
              completedAt?: string;
              forkedFrom?: { atTurn: number; parentRunId: string };
              mode: "turn-loop" | "batch-trajectory" | "batch-point";
              runId: string;
              scenario: { id: string; name: string; version?: string };
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              seed?: number;
              startedAt: string;
          }

          Required. Identifies the run + scenario + mode.

          • OptionalcompletedAt?: string
          • OptionalforkedFrom?: { atTurn: number; parentRunId: string }

            When this run was produced by forking a prior run, records the parent run-id + the turn at which the fork happened. Consumers walking a fork chain follow this link transitively through stored artifacts. Omitted for fresh (non-forked) runs.

            Added in 0.7.x with the WorldModel.fork() API. Additive + optional; no COMPILE_SCHEMA_VERSION bump required.

          • mode: "turn-loop" | "batch-trajectory" | "batch-point"
          • runId: string

            Unique identifier for this run. UUID, slug, or host-assigned id.

          • scenario: { id: string; name: string; version?: string }
            • id: string
            • name: string
            • Optionalversion?: string

              Scenario version; not artifact version. Optional.

          • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
          • Optionalseed?: number

            Absent for non-deterministic runs (LLM-only synthesis without a seeded kernel).

          • startedAt: string
        • Optionaloverview?: string

          Short headline summary. Digital-twin overview, paracosm verdict's summary, a game's end-of-run narrator line.

        • OptionalproviderError?:
              | {
                  actionUrl?: string;
                  kind: "unknown"
                  | "auth"
                  | "quota"
                  | "rate_limit"
                  | "network";
                  message: string;
                  provider: string;
              }
              | null
        • OptionalriskFlags?: {
              detail: string;
              label: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              severity: "low" | "medium" | "high";
          }[]

          Risk callouts. Maps to digital-twin risk_flags after camelCase rename.

        • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
        • OptionalspecialistNotes?: {
              confidence?: number;
              detail?: {
                  citations?: {
                      context: string;
                      doi?: string;
                      text: string;
                      url: string;
                  }[];
                  openQuestions?: string[];
                  opportunities?: { description: string; impact: "low"
                  | "medium"
                  | "high" }[];
                  recommendedActions?: string[];
                  risks?: {
                      description: string;
                      severity: "low" | "medium" | "high" | "critical";
                  }[];
              };
              domain: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              summary: string;
              trajectory?: "positive" | "negative" | "neutral" | "mixed";
          }[]

          Specialist analyses across domains. Flat list; multiple entries per domain/turn OK.

        • Optionalsubject?: {
              conditions?: string[];
              id: string;
              markers?: {
                  category?: string;
                  id: string;
                  interpretation?: string;
                  value?: string;
              }[];
              name: string;
              personality?: Record<string, number>;
              profile?: Record<string, unknown>;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              signals?: {
                  label: string;
                  recordedAt?: string;
                  unit?: string;
                  value: string | number;
              }[];
          }

          Subject being simulated (person, character, organism, vessel, etc.).

        • Optionaltrajectory?: {
              points?: {
                  metrics: Record<string, number>;
                  scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                  time: number;
              }[];
              timepoints?: {
                  confidence?: number;
                  highlightMetrics?: {
                      color?: string;
                      direction?: "up"
                      | "down"
                      | "stable";
                      label: string;
                      value: string;
                  }[];
                  label: string;
                  narrative?: string;
                  reasoning?: string;
                  scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                  score?: { label: string; max: number; min: number; value: number };
                  time: number;
                  worldSnapshot?: {
                      capacities?: Record<string, number>;
                      environment?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                      metrics: Record<string, number>;
                      politics?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                      scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                      statuses?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                  };
              }[];
              timeUnit: { plural: string; singular: string };
          }

          Labeled trajectory of the simulation. trajectory.points for sparklines, trajectory.timepoints for rich labeled snapshots. Both optional; at least one populated in turn-loop and batch-trajectory modes.

          • Optionalpoints?: {
                metrics: Record<string, number>;
                scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                time: number;
            }[]

            Lightweight per-sample metric records; good for sparklines.

          • Optionaltimepoints?: {
                confidence?: number;
                highlightMetrics?: {
                    color?: string;
                    direction?: "up" | "down" | "stable";
                    label: string;
                    value: string;
                }[];
                label: string;
                narrative?: string;
                reasoning?: string;
                scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;

                time: number;
                worldSnapshot?: {
                    capacities?: Record<string, number>;
                    environment?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                    metrics: Record<string, number>;
                    politics?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                    scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                    statuses?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                };
            }[]

            Rich labeled snapshots with narrative + score; good for timepoint cards.

          • timeUnit: { plural: string; singular: string }

      • {

        • agents: {
              age?: number;
              agentId: string;
              alive: boolean;
              childrenIds?: string[];
              department: string;
              featured?: boolean;
              generation?: number;
              marsborn?: boolean;
              mood?: string;
              name: string;
              partnerId?: string;
              psychScore?: number;
              rank?: "junior" | "senior" | "lead" | "chief";
              role: string;
              shortTermMemory?: string[];
          }[]

          Every agent in the swarm at snapshot time, alive or dead.

        • Optionalbirths?: number

          Number of births this turn.

        • Optionaldeaths?: number

          Number of deaths this turn.

        • Optionalmorale?: number

          Aggregate morale 0..1 (population-weighted).

        • population: number

          Aggregate counts derived from agents.

        • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
        • time: number

          Scenario time at snapshot (years/quarters/ticks per scenario).

        • turn: number

          Turn number this snapshot was taken at (0-indexed).

      • undefined

      Example

      const result = await wm.simulate(leader, { maxTurns: 6 });
      const swarm = WorldModel.swarm(result);
      if (swarm) {
        console.log(`T${swarm.turn}: ${swarm.population} agents`);
        for (const a of swarm.agents) console.log(`  ${a.name} · ${a.department} · ${a.mood}`);
      }
      • Defined in runtime/world-model/index.ts:328

                          scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                          statuses?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                      };
                  }[];
                  timeUnit: { plural: string; singular: string };
              };
          },
      ): Record<string, SwarmAgent[]>

      Group the swarm by department. Returns a map keyed by department label; values are the (alive + dead) agents in that department, preserving insertion order from the snapshot. Delegates to import('paracosm/swarm').swarmByDepartment.

      Useful for org-chart-style summaries: "Engineering: 18 agents (15 alive). Lead: Maria Chen."

      Parameters

      • artifact: {
            aborted?: boolean;
            assumptions?: string[];
            citations?: { context: string; doi?: string; text: string; url: string }[];
            cost?: {
                breakdown?: Record<string, number>;
                cachedReadTokens?: number;
                cacheSavingsUSD?: number;
                inputTokens?: number;
                llmCalls?: number;
                outputTokens?: number;
                totalUSD: number;
            };
            decisions?: {
                actor?: string;
                choice: string;
                outcome?: | "risky_success"
                | "risky_failure"
                | "conservative_success"
                | "conservative_failure";
                rationale?: string;
                reasoning?: string;
                scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                time: number;

        • Optionalaborted?: boolean
        • Optionalassumptions?: string[]

          Assumptions held true during the simulation.

        • Optionalcitations?: { context: string; doi?: string; text: string; url: string }[]
        • Optionalcost?: {
              breakdown?: Record<string, number>;
              cachedReadTokens?: number;
              cacheSavingsUSD?: number;
              inputTokens?: number;
              llmCalls?: number;
              outputTokens?: number;
              totalUSD: number;
          }
          • Optionalbreakdown?: Record<string, number>

            Per-site / per-model breakdown: { director: 0.12, departments: 0.34 }.

          • OptionalcachedReadTokens?: number
          • OptionalcacheSavingsUSD?: number
          • OptionalinputTokens?: number
          • OptionalllmCalls?: number
          • OptionaloutputTokens?: number
          • totalUSD: number
        • Optionaldecisions?: {
              actor?: string;
              choice: string;
              outcome?:
                  | "risky_success"
                  | "risky_failure"
                  | "conservative_success"
                  | "conservative_failure";
              rationale?: string;
              reasoning?: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              time: number;
          }[]

          Every decision made during the run — one per commander choice in turn-loop.

        • Optionaldisclaimer?: string

          Scenario-supplied disclaimer (digital-twin simulations use this for medical / professional caveats).

        • OptionalfinalState?: {
              capacities?: Record<string, number>;
              environment?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>;
              metrics: Record<string, number>;
              politics?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              statuses?: Record<string, string | boolean>;
          }
          • Optionalcapacities?: Record<string, number>

            Capacity constraints: life support, housing, budget.

          • Optionalenvironment?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>

            Environmental conditions: weather, radiation, depth, altitude.

          • metrics: Record<string, number>

            Numeric gauges: food, power, population, morale, VO2max, whatever.

          • Optionalpolitics?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>

            Political/social pressures. Values may be numeric or categorical.

          • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
          • Optionalstatuses?: Record<string, string | boolean>

            Categorical state: governance status, alignment, phase.

        • OptionalfinalSwarm?: {
              agents: {
                  age?: number;
                  agentId: string;
                  alive: boolean;
                  childrenIds?: string[];
                  department: string;
                  featured?: boolean;
                  generation?: number;
                  marsborn?: boolean;
                  mood?: string;
                  name: string;
                  partnerId?: string;
                  psychScore?: number;
                  rank?: "junior" | "senior" | "lead" | "chief";
                  role: string;
                  shortTermMemory?: string[];
              }[];
              births?: number;
              deaths?: number;
              morale?: number;
              population: number;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              time: number;
              turn: number;
          }

          Final agent-swarm roster at end-of-run. Pairs with finalState: the world snapshot is the macro view (metrics, statuses), the swarm snapshot is the micro view (every agent's role, mood, family edges, memory). Populated in turn-loop runs that exercised the swarm. Use WorldModel.swarm() for the live equivalent during a run.

          • agents: {
                age?: number;
                agentId: string;
                alive: boolean;
                childrenIds?: string[];
                department: string;
                featured?: boolean;
                generation?: number;
                marsborn?: boolean;
                mood?: string;
                name: string;
                partnerId?: string;
                psychScore?: number;
                rank?: "junior" | "senior" | "lead" | "chief";
                role: string;
                shortTermMemory?: string[];
            }[]

            Every agent in the swarm at snapshot time, alive or dead.

          • Optionalbirths?: number

            Number of births this turn.

          • Optionaldeaths?: number

            Number of deaths this turn.

          • Optionalmorale?: number

            Aggregate morale 0..1 (population-weighted).

          • population: number

            Aggregate counts derived from agents.

          • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
          • time: number

            Scenario time at snapshot (years/quarters/ticks per scenario).

          • turn: number

            Turn number this snapshot was taken at (0-indexed).

        • Optionalfingerprint?: Record<string, string | number>

          Loose classification scores. Paracosm heritage; scenarios may extend.

        • OptionalforgedTools?: {
              approved: boolean;
              confidence?: number;
              department?: string;
              description?: string;
              name: string;
          }[]
        • Optionalintervention?: {
              adherenceProfile?: { expected: number; risks?: string[] };
              category?: string;
              description: string;
              duration?: { unit: string; value: number };
              id: string;
              mechanism?: string;
              name: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              targetBehaviors?: string[];
          }

          Intervention being tested on the subject.

        • OptionalleveragePoints?: string[]

          Actionable leverage points for consumers of the artifact.

        • metadata: {
              completedAt?: string;
              forkedFrom?: { atTurn: number; parentRunId: string };
              mode: "turn-loop" | "batch-trajectory" | "batch-point";
              runId: string;
              scenario: { id: string; name: string; version?: string };
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              seed?: number;
              startedAt: string;
          }

          Required. Identifies the run + scenario + mode.

          • OptionalcompletedAt?: string
          • OptionalforkedFrom?: { atTurn: number; parentRunId: string }

            When this run was produced by forking a prior run, records the parent run-id + the turn at which the fork happened. Consumers walking a fork chain follow this link transitively through stored artifacts. Omitted for fresh (non-forked) runs.

            Added in 0.7.x with the WorldModel.fork() API. Additive + optional; no COMPILE_SCHEMA_VERSION bump required.

          • mode: "turn-loop" | "batch-trajectory" | "batch-point"
          • runId: string

            Unique identifier for this run. UUID, slug, or host-assigned id.

          • scenario: { id: string; name: string; version?: string }
            • id: string
            • name: string
            • Optionalversion?: string

              Scenario version; not artifact version. Optional.

          • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
          • Optionalseed?: number

            Absent for non-deterministic runs (LLM-only synthesis without a seeded kernel).

          • startedAt: string
        • Optionaloverview?: string

          Short headline summary. Digital-twin overview, paracosm verdict's summary, a game's end-of-run narrator line.

        • OptionalproviderError?:
              | {
                  actionUrl?: string;
                  kind: "unknown"
                  | "auth"
                  | "quota"
                  | "rate_limit"
                  | "network";
                  message: string;
                  provider: string;
              }
              | null
        • OptionalriskFlags?: {
              detail: string;
              label: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              severity: "low" | "medium" | "high";
          }[]

          Risk callouts. Maps to digital-twin risk_flags after camelCase rename.

        • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
        • OptionalspecialistNotes?: {
              confidence?: number;
              detail?: {
                  citations?: {
                      context: string;
                      doi?: string;
                      text: string;
                      url: string;
                  }[];
                  openQuestions?: string[];
                  opportunities?: { description: string; impact: "low"
                  | "medium"
                  | "high" }[];
                  recommendedActions?: string[];
                  risks?: {
                      description: string;
                      severity: "low" | "medium" | "high" | "critical";
                  }[];
              };
              domain: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              summary: string;
              trajectory?: "positive" | "negative" | "neutral" | "mixed";
          }[]

          Specialist analyses across domains. Flat list; multiple entries per domain/turn OK.

        • Optionalsubject?: {
              conditions?: string[];
              id: string;
              markers?: {
                  category?: string;
                  id: string;
                  interpretation?: string;
                  value?: string;
              }[];
              name: string;
              personality?: Record<string, number>;
              profile?: Record<string, unknown>;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              signals?: {
                  label: string;
                  recordedAt?: string;
                  unit?: string;
                  value: string | number;
              }[];
          }

          Subject being simulated (person, character, organism, vessel, etc.).

        • Optionaltrajectory?: {
              points?: {
                  metrics: Record<string, number>;
                  scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                  time: number;
              }[];
              timepoints?: {
                  confidence?: number;
                  highlightMetrics?: {
                      color?: string;
                      direction?: "up"
                      | "down"
                      | "stable";
                      label: string;
                      value: string;
                  }[];
                  label: string;
                  narrative?: string;
                  reasoning?: string;
                  scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                  score?: { label: string; max: number; min: number; value: number };
                  time: number;
                  worldSnapshot?: {
                      capacities?: Record<string, number>;
                      environment?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                      metrics: Record<string, number>;
                      politics?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                      scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                      statuses?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                  };
              }[];
              timeUnit: { plural: string; singular: string };
          }

          Labeled trajectory of the simulation. trajectory.points for sparklines, trajectory.timepoints for rich labeled snapshots. Both optional; at least one populated in turn-loop and batch-trajectory modes.

          • Optionalpoints?: {
                metrics: Record<string, number>;
                scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                time: number;
            }[]

            Lightweight per-sample metric records; good for sparklines.

          • Optionaltimepoints?: {
                confidence?: number;
                highlightMetrics?: {
                    color?: string;
                    direction?: "up" | "down" | "stable";
                    label: string;
                    value: string;
                }[];
                label: string;
                narrative?: string;
                reasoning?: string;
                scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                score?: { label: string; max: number; min: number; value: number };
                time: number;
                worldSnapshot?: {
                    capacities?: Record<string, number>;
                    environment?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                    metrics: Record<string, number>;
                    politics?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                    scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                    statuses?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                };
            }[]

            Rich labeled snapshots with narrative + score; good for timepoint cards.

          • timeUnit: { plural: string; singular: string }

      Returns Record<string, SwarmAgent[]>

      • Defined in runtime/world-model/index.ts:341

                  points?: {
                      metrics: Record<string, number>;
                      scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                      time: number;
                  }[];
                  timepoints?: {
                      confidence?: number;
                      highlightMetrics?: {
                          color?: string;
                          direction?: "up"
                          | "down"
                          | "stable";
                          label: string;
                          value: string;
                      }[];
                      label: string;
                      narrative?: string;
                      reasoning?: string;
                      scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                      score?: { label: string; max: number; min: number; value: number };
                      time: number;
                      worldSnapshot?: {
                          capacities?: Record<string, number>;
                          environment?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                          metrics: Record<string, number>;
                          politics?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                          scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                          statuses?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                      };
                  }[];
                  timeUnit: { plural: string; singular: string };
              };
          },
      ): Record<string, string[]>

      Build a family-tree adjacency map from the swarm: parent agentId → list of direct-descendant agentIds. Edge direction is parent→child; walk the map recursively to render multi-generation trees. Founders (no parent in the swarm) are the roots. Delegates to import('paracosm/swarm').swarmFamilyTree.

      Returns an empty object when the run produced no swarm or the scenario does not track family edges.

      Parameters

      • artifact: {
            aborted?: boolean;
            assumptions?: string[];
            citations?: { context: string; doi?: string; text: string; url: string }[];
            cost?: {
                breakdown?: Record<string, number>;
                cachedReadTokens?: number;
                cacheSavingsUSD?: number;
                inputTokens?: number;

        • Optionalaborted?: boolean
        • Optionalassumptions?: string[]

          Assumptions held true during the simulation.

            Per-site / per-model breakdown: { director: 0.12, departments: 0.34 }.

          • OptionalinputTokens?: number

              rationale?: string;
              reasoning?: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              time: number;
          }[]

          Every decision made during the run — one per commander choice in turn-loop.

        • Optionaldisclaimer?: string

          Scenario-supplied disclaimer (digital-twin simulations use this for medical / professional caveats).

        • OptionalfinalState?: {
              capacities?: Record<string, number>;
              environment?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>;
              metrics: Record<string, number>;
              politics?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              statuses?: Record<string, string | boolean>;
          }
          • Optionalcapacities?: Record<string, number>

            Capacity constraints: life support, housing, budget.

          • Optionalenvironment?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>

            Environmental conditions: weather, radiation, depth, altitude.

          • metrics: Record<string, number>

            Numeric gauges: food, power, population, morale, VO2max, whatever.

          • Optionalpolitics?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>

            Political/social pressures. Values may be numeric or categorical.

          • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
          • Optionalstatuses?: Record<string, string | boolean>

            Categorical state: governance status, alignment, phase.

        • OptionalfinalSwarm?: {
              agents: {
                  age?: number;
                  agentId: string;
                  alive: boolean;
                  childrenIds?: string[];
                  department: string;
                  featured?: boolean;
                  generation?: number;
                  marsborn?: boolean;
                  mood?: string;
                  name: string;
                  partnerId?: string;
                  psychScore?: number;
                  rank?: "junior" | "senior" | "lead" | "chief";
                  role: string;
                  shortTermMemory?: string[];
              }[];
              births?: number;
              deaths?: number;
              morale?: number;
              population: number;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              time: number;
              turn: number;
          }

          Final agent-swarm roster at end-of-run. Pairs with finalState: the world snapshot is the macro view (metrics, statuses), the swarm snapshot is the micro view (every agent's role, mood, family edges, memory). Populated in turn-loop runs that exercised the swarm. Use WorldModel.swarm() for the live equivalent during a run.

          • agents: {
                age?: number;
                agentId: string;
                alive: boolean;
                childrenIds?: string[];
                department: string;
                featured?: boolean;
                generation?: number;
                marsborn?: boolean;
                mood?: string;
                name: string;
                partnerId?: string;
                psychScore?: number;
                rank?: "junior" | "senior" | "lead" | "chief";
                role: string;
                shortTermMemory?: string[];
            }[]

            Every agent in the swarm at snapshot time, alive or dead.

          • Optionalbirths?: number

            Number of births this turn.

          • Optionaldeaths?: number

            Number of deaths this turn.

          • Optionalmorale?: number

            Aggregate morale 0..1 (population-weighted).

          • population: number

            Aggregate counts derived from agents.

          • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
          • time: number

            Scenario time at snapshot (years/quarters/ticks per scenario).

          • turn: number

            Turn number this snapshot was taken at (0-indexed).

        • Optionalfingerprint?: Record<string, string | number>

          Loose classification scores. Paracosm heritage; scenarios may extend.

        • OptionalforgedTools?: {
              approved: boolean;
              confidence?: number;
              department?: string;
              description?: string;
              name: string;
          }[]
        • Optionalintervention?: {
              adherenceProfile?: { expected: number; risks?: string[] };
              category?: string;
              description: string;
              duration?: { unit: string; value: number };
              id: string;
              mechanism?: string;
              name: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              targetBehaviors?: string[];
          }

          Intervention being tested on the subject.

        • OptionalleveragePoints?: string[]

          Actionable leverage points for consumers of the artifact.

        • metadata: {
              completedAt?: string;
              forkedFrom?: { atTurn: number; parentRunId: string };
              mode: "turn-loop" | "batch-trajectory" | "batch-point";
              runId: string;
              scenario: { id: string; name: string; version?: string };
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              seed?: number;
              startedAt: string;
          }

          Required. Identifies the run + scenario + mode.

          • OptionalcompletedAt?: string
          • OptionalforkedFrom?: { atTurn: number; parentRunId: string }

            When this run was produced by forking a prior run, records the parent run-id + the turn at which the fork happened. Consumers walking a fork chain follow this link transitively through stored artifacts. Omitted for fresh (non-forked) runs.

            Added in 0.7.x with the WorldModel.fork() API. Additive + optional; no COMPILE_SCHEMA_VERSION bump required.

          • mode: "turn-loop" | "batch-trajectory" | "batch-point"
          • runId: string

            Unique identifier for this run. UUID, slug, or host-assigned id.

          • scenario: { id: string; name: string; version?: string }
            • id: string
            • name: string
            • Optionalversion?: string

              Scenario version; not artifact version. Optional.

          • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
          • Optionalseed?: number

            Absent for non-deterministic runs (LLM-only synthesis without a seeded kernel).

          • startedAt: string
        • Optionaloverview?: string

          Short headline summary. Digital-twin overview, paracosm verdict's summary, a game's end-of-run narrator line.

        • OptionalproviderError?:
              | {
                  actionUrl?: string;
                  kind: "unknown"
                  | "auth"
                  | "quota"
                  | "rate_limit"
                  | "network";
                  message: string;
                  provider: string;
              }
              | null
        • OptionalriskFlags?: {
              detail: string;
              label: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              severity: "low" | "medium" | "high";
          }[]

          Risk callouts. Maps to digital-twin risk_flags after camelCase rename.

        • OptionalscenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>
        • OptionalspecialistNotes?: {
              confidence?: number;
              detail?: {
                  citations?: {
                      context: string;
                      doi?: string;
                      text: string;
                      url: string;
                  }[];
                  openQuestions?: string[];
                  opportunities?: { description: string; impact: "low"
                  | "medium"
                  | "high" }[];
                  recommendedActions?: string[];
                  risks?: {
                      description: string;
                      severity: "low" | "medium" | "high" | "critical";
                  }[];
              };
              domain: string;
              scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
              summary: string;
              trajectory?: "positive" | "negative" | "neutral" | "mixed";
          }[]

          Specialist analyses across domains. Flat list; multiple entries per domain/turn OK.

        • Optionalsubject?: {
              conditions?: string[];
              id: string;
              markers?: {
                  category?: string;
                  id: string;
                  interpretation?: string;
                  value?: string;
              }[];

              signals?: {
                  label: string;
                  recordedAt?: string;
                  unit?: string;

              }[];
          }

          Subject being simulated (person, character, organism, vessel, etc.).

        • Optionaltrajectory?: {
              points?: {
                  metrics: Record<string, number>;
                  scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                  time: number;
              }[];
              timepoints?: {

                  }[];

                  narrative?: string;
                  reasoning?: string;
                  scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                  score?: { label: string; max: number; min: number; value: number };
                  time: number;
                  worldSnapshot?: {
                      capacities?: Record<string, number>;
                      environment?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                      metrics: Record<string, number>;
                      politics?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                      scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                      statuses?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                  };
              }[];
              timeUnit: { plural: string; singular: string };
          }

          Labeled trajectory of the simulation. trajectory.points for sparklines, trajectory.timepoints for rich labeled snapshots. Both optional; at least one populated in turn-loop and batch-trajectory modes.

          • Optionalpoints?: {
                metrics: Record<string, number>;
                scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                time: number;
            }[]

            Lightweight per-sample metric records; good for sparklines.

          • Optionaltimepoints?: {
                confidence?: number;
                highlightMetrics?: {
                    color?: string;
                    direction?: "up" | "down" | "stable";
                    label: string;
                    value: string;
                }[];
                label: string;
                narrative?: string;
                reasoning?: string;
                scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                score?: { label: string; max: number; min: number; value: number };
                time: number;
                worldSnapshot?: {

                    environment?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                    metrics: Record<string, number>;
                    politics?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                    scenarioExtensions?: Record<string, unknown>;
                    statuses?: Record<string, (...) | (...) | (...)>;
                };
            }[]

            Rich labeled snapshots with narrative + score; good for timepoint cards.

          • timeUnit: { plural: string; singular: string }

      Returns Record<string, string[]>

      • Defined in runtime/world-model/index.ts:355

    Properties

    Readonlyscenario

    scenario: ScenarioPackage

    The underlying compiled scenario. Exposed so callers can reuse the same compiled package across custom integrations.

    • Defined in runtime/world-model/index.ts:211
